pullRequest.title: Fix UDP multicast interface on MacOS
pullRequest.body:
Hi, 

This fixes https://github.com/hardbyte/python-can/issues/1760 to allow usage of the UDP Multicast backend on MacOS. 

The ioctl check before timestamping in recv was only partially fixed for Windows on main, but MacOS still doesn't support this ioctl. 

The second change is adding the SO_REUSEPORT option.

pullRequest.title: Keep Track of Active Notifiers. Make Notifier usable as ContextManager.
pullRequest.body:
- Make Notifier usable as a context manager to automatically call `Notifier.stop()`. Adapt examples to use `with can.Notifier(...)`
- Add a NotifierRegistry class to ensure that a bus is not added to multiple active Notifiers
- Fixes #1888

pullRequest.title: Keep a reference to asyncio tasks
pullRequest.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to python-can!
Please fill out the following template to help us review your pull request.
-->

## Summary of Changes

In `can.Notifier._on_message_received()` save a reference of each created `asyncio.Task` into a set and automatically discard it once it is fnished.

pullRequest.title: Fix/asc writer timestamps format
pullRequest.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to python-can!
Please fill out the following template to help us review your pull request.
-->

## Summary of Changes

<!-- Briefly describe what your pull request does. -->

  - Added `timestamps_format` parameter to `ASCWriter.__init__()` (`"absolute"` or `"relative"`, default `"absolute"`)                                                              
  - The value is written into the `base hex  timestamps ...` header line so external tools (CANalyzer, CANoe, etc.) can interpret the file correctly
  - Raises `ValueError` for unsupported values                                                                                                                                      
  - No behaviour change for existing code — default is `"absolute"`

## Related Issues / Pull Requests

<!-- List any related issues, pull requests, or discussions. -->

- Closes #2022 

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactoring
- [ ] Other (please describe):

## Checklist

- [x] I have added or updated documentation as appropriate.
- [x] I have followed the [contribution guide](https://python-can.readthedocs.io/en/main/development.html).
- [x] I have added or updated tests as appropriate.
- [x] I have added a [news fragment](doc/changelog.d/) for towncrier.
- [x] All checks and tests pass (`tox`).

## Additional Notes
  The `timestamps_format` parameter only affects the header line.
  The actual per-message timestamp values written by `on_message_received()`
  are not modified — callers are responsible for passing appropriately scaled timestamps.

<!-- Add any other information or context you want to share. -->

pullRequest.title: Changed slcan serial read routine to be faster and to manage the buffer more carefully
pullRequest.body:
## Summary of Changes
Changes the serial read in slcan to be faster by reading all available bytes at once rather than one at a time. This is a huge performance boost (at least on windows).

## Related Issues / Pull Requests

- Closes #2027
- Potentially related to #1746
- Potentially related to #1701 (buffer management issue)
- Potentially related to #1541
